metadata.title: Professional Headshot Generator
metadata.description: Create professional AI headshots with a guided tool dashboard, usage instructions, and practical photo preparation advice.

result.tip_text: Not happy with the presets? Upload a reference image and update the description.

overview.title: Professional Headshot Full Guide
overview.description: A strong professional headshot depends on expression, lighting, composition, scene fit, and export quality working together.

items.title: 1. Core Golden Rule: Make The Three Elements Work Together
items.description: A successful professional headshot is built from expression, lighting, and composition working as one system. Keep the smile natural: a teeth-showing smile usually feels more approachable for business profiles, while a closed-mouth smile can feel more confident and authoritative. Avoid harsh top lighting that creates dark eye sockets. Use a ring light or soft side lighting to add dimension to the face. For composition, place the eyes around the upper third of the frame, avoid excessive empty space above the head, and never crop the top of the hairline.

items.title: 2. Scene Fit Matters More Than Most People Expect
items.description: Different platforms need different visual language. For LinkedIn and resumes, use neat dark hair styling or tied-back hair, with a blazer, shirt, or business outfit against gray, light blue, or a softly blurred office background. For internal company tools like Slack, use softer hair styling, natural loose hair, and clean knitwear or premium T-shirts with bright indoor backgrounds. For creator personal brands, trend-driven hair color such as cool brown or muted ash tones can work well with design-forward hoodies, accessories, and minimal gradient backgrounds. For academic or medical use, classic dark hair, clean short styling, and formal shirts or medical attire work best against white or light beige backgrounds.

items.title: 3. Export Specs Make The Result Usable, Not Toy-Like
items.description: Use the right output specs so the image works as a real productivity asset. Recommended minimums: LinkedIn at 400 x 400 px, Gmail or Slack at 250 x 250 px, and 1024 x 1024 px or higher for high-density displays. Use PNG when you need maximum clarity or transparent backgrounds. Use WebP when the image is meant for personal websites and you want a smaller file size with good quality. Export in sRGB so colors stay consistent across browsers, phones, and desktop displays.

requirements.title: How to Create an Upload-Ready Professional Headshot Base Image
requirements.description: To generate a high-quality result, the base photo matters. Follow the steps below to prepare a clear portrait image for better AI output.

items.title: 1. Outfit and Appearance
items.description: Choose clean, business-style clothing such as a light shirt with a dark jacket. Keep your hair neat, makeup and accessories simple, reduce facial shine, and avoid strong glare on glasses. The goal is a clean, natural look.

items.title: 2. Choose the Right Light
items.description: Stand in front of a window and face soft natural light. Overcast daylight or filtered window light works best. Avoid direct sun, harsh shadows, and mixed indoor lighting that can make skin tones look uneven.

items.title: 3. Keep the Background Simple
items.description: Use a plain wall or a simple indoor background with some contrast from your clothing. Avoid cluttered spaces or backgrounds that blend into your hair and outfit. A simple background helps the AI read your outline more accurately.

items.title: 4. Use the Right Shooting Angle
items.description: Use the main camera on your phone or camera and keep it at eye level. Relax your shoulders, lean slightly forward, and keep a natural expression. Frame your head, shoulders, and upper body with a little space above the head and no cropped shoulders.

items.title: 5. Take Multiple Options
items.description: Take at least ten photos before choosing one to upload. Try a soft smile, a neutral expression, small angle changes, and slightly different light positions. More options give you a better chance of finding the ideal base image.

items.title: 6. Check Before Upload
items.description: Make sure the image is sharp, evenly lit, centered, and clean in the background. Confirm the shoulders are visible and the face is clearly in focus. A photo that meets these conditions is a strong base image for generation.

faq.title: Professional headshot questions
faq.description: Common questions about uploading, generating, and using your professional headshot.

items.question: What kind of photo should I upload?
items.answer: Upload a clear front-facing portrait with even lighting and visible shoulders. A simple background and sharp facial details will usually produce a better professional headshot.

items.question: Do I need to write a description?
items.answer: No. You can generate a result with the default style settings. If you want more control, add a short description for outfit, background, lighting, or expression.

items.question: How long does it take to generate a headshot?
items.answer: Generation usually takes a few seconds to a short wait, depending on image size, selected settings, and current traffic.

items.question: Where can I use the generated headshot?
items.answer: You can use it for LinkedIn, resumes, personal websites, team profile pages, business cards, and other professional profile placements.

items.question: What should I do if the result does not look right?
items.answer: Try uploading a clearer photo, choosing a different style, or refining the description. Small changes to lighting, background, and expression instructions can improve the final result.
